element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn different enemy patterns and attack timings
Culmination of level mechanics; requires pattern recognition
Screen-clearing mechanic for crowd control; limited use resource
Enables two-player simultaneous play; changes pacing and tactics

typical_relief_triggers: Using special attack to clear crowd
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Rapid, wave-based combat with escalating enemy density leading to boss climax at stage end; repeating cycle across multiple stages

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction to basic melee combat and enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing enemy variety and density; more aggressive boss mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: High enemy spawn rates; complex boss patterns; resource management critical

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage progresses from initial enemy encounters through escalating waves to a final boss battle; linear left-to-right progression
Enemies spawn in organized waves rather than continuously; players get brief relief between waves before next spawn
Crime lord and affiliated boss characters serve as stage-end encounters; require learned attack patterns to defeat

notes: TCRF source was not used due to explicit restriction on summarizing that content. Data extracted from Wikipedia article on Streets of Rage series, specifically information about the original 1991 Genesis release, its gameplay mechanics (Shinobi-style special attack), and basic structure. More detailed level-by-level sequencing data would require additional game-specific design documentation.
